alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google with two promotions, scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs; demonstrated ownership of requirements analysis, UAT, rollout and programme maturation
alignments.job_requirement: Act decisively with minimal guidance, own outcomes end-to-end and initiate solutions without waiting for direction

alignments.candidate_evidence: Advanced Excel and Google Sheets; Looker dashboard building; demonstrated ability to translate complex system architectures into structured visual outputs
alignments.job_requirement: Create clear isometric line art, block diagrams and system flowcharts derived from SolidWorks or electrical schematics

alignments.candidate_evidence: Participated directly in design reviews, UAT and hardware/software validation during transformation programmes at Google; experience working with multidisciplinary engineering teams
alignments.job_requirement: Participate in design reviews, validation events and hands-on hardware integration sessions

gaps.gap: No aerospace, defence, robotics or UAV domain background; Commercial Operations and Product Operations experience does not translate to physical product technical documentation in regulated environments
gaps.mitigation: Candidate would require substantial domain ramp (6–12 months minimum) to build fluency in aerospace/defence terminology, regulatory frameworks, compliance standards, and flight-safety procedures. Domain risk is not easily mitigated by operations experience alone.

gaps.gap: No demonstrated expertise with structured technical authoring tools (MadCap Flare, Adobe FrameMaker, LaTeX); no PLM (Windchill, Arena) or version-control (Git, equivalent) administration experience
gaps.mitigation: These tools are specialist and highly domain-integrated. Candidate has advanced spreadsheet and basic BI skills but no evidence of learning or adopting enterprise technical authoring or PLM platforms.

gaps.gap: No hands-on CAD (SolidWorks, NX), electrical schematic, PCB layout, or hardware integration experience; cannot independently read and interpret 3D assemblies or electrical wire harnesses
gaps.mitigation: This is a core job function requiring first-principles ability to extract intent from engineering artefacts. Candidate's experience is operational and strategic, not hardware or electrical domain knowledge.

gaps.gap: No technical writing background, certification or portfolio; no experience authoring Interface Control Documents, System Architecture Descriptions, Operator Manuals or Maintenance Guides
gaps.mitigation: Technical writing is a distinct craft requiring structured methodology, traceability discipline, and compliance thinking. No evidence that candidate has developed or supervised technical writing workflows.

gaps.gap: Limited evidence of working directly with mechanical, electrical or aerodynamics engineers on detailed system documentation; Commercial Operations experience is not equivalent to multidisciplinary technical authorship
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has cross-functional collaboration skills but in operational/commercial context. Physical product documentation collaboration requires deep technical engagement and subject-matter fluency that is not demonstrated.

output.level_rationale: Physical product technical writing role requiring multidisciplinary aerospace/defence/UAV domain expertise, direct CAD/schematics interpretation, structured authoring tools (MadCap, FrameMaker, LaTeX), and PLM/version-control fluency. Implies 5–8 years of specialist technical documentation experience. ownership of end-to-end documentation maturity and compliance suggests mid-senior IC scope (L4 floor). Company stage unknown (early/growth implied by European mission and hiring velocity). reporting line not stated.
output.summary: Systems Technical Writer at Helsing is a specialist physical-product documentation role in aerospace/defence requiring deep domain fluency (CAD, schematics, aerodynamics), hands-on hardware integration, and proficiency in enterprise technical authoring tools (MadCap Flare, PLM platforms). Candidate brings strong ownership, multidisciplinary collaboration and complex translation skills from eight years in Commercial/Product Operations at Google, but has no aerospace, defence, robotics, UAV, or technical-writing background; no hands-on CAD or electrical domain knowledge; and no structured technical authoring (MadCap, FrameMaker, PLM) experience. Domain gap and specialist tool/craft deficit are not easily closed and fall substantially outside Strategy and Operations / Product Operations families. Advisory: skip.
uncertainties: Company size and stage not stated; growth-stage or scaling phase implied but unknown funding, headcount, reporting structure
Specific reporting line, management scope, and team size not stated; assumed individual-contributor mid-senior scope based on 'direct collaboration' and 'end-to-end ownership' language
Candidate's experience with CAD-adjacent work (e.g., requirements traceability, integration oversight) not explicitly ruled out but not demonstrated in profile; unclear whether any exposure exists
